title: Firmware Non-Compliance Detection (Running vs Golden Image)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detecting devices that are not on the approved golden image is direct evidence of drift from the established baseline configuration for network infrastructure.
compliance.controlObjective: Identifies when managed Catalyst devices run firmware outside the defined golden image so teams can realign the fleet with the organization‚Äôs baseline.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search or alert on UC-5.13.56 with query history and tickets when imageCompliance is non-compliant; optional export to GRC for CM-2 follow-up.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Highlighting non-golden software supports flaw remediation and upgrade planning, which is a necessary input to consistent patch and firmware management.
compliance.controlObjective: Surfaces which devices must be updated or restandardized so flaw-remediation and upgrade work can be prioritized for SI-2 alignment.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Monthly report of non-compliant device counts by `deviceFamily` and `runningVersion` vs `targetVersion` from Splunk, linked to change tickets.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md ¬ß Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `imageCompliance` (string), `runningVersion` (string), `targetVersion` (string), `deviceFamily` (string), `deviceName` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `upgradeStatus` (string). CIM data model: Change.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for SWIM API
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" imageCompliance="NON_COMPLIANT" | stats count as non_compliant_count by deviceFamily, runningVersion, targetVersion | eval version_gap=if(isnotnull(runningVersion) AND isnotnull(targetVersion), tostring(runningVersion)." ‚Üí ".tostring(targetVersion), null()) | sort -non_compliant_count
description: Identifies devices running software versions that do not match the Catalyst Center golden image assignment, indicating firmware non-compliance.
value: Devices running non-golden images may have known vulnerabilities, missing features, or untested configurations. Firmware compliance is a key security control.
implementation:
Use the same SWIM scripted input as UC-5.13.55 (`sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`), ensuring the collector merges **image compliance** with **running** and **target** version fields. Poll at minimum: `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` and `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` so each event can carry `imageCompliance`, `runningVersion`, and `targetVersion` (golden). Authenticate with `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token`. Map Catalyst Center‚Äôs compliance status values so `imageCompliance=NON_COMPLIANT` (or your deployment‚Äôs equivalent string) is normalized in Splunk. Optional: enrich with HEC if you emit compliance-change events in real time.

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_swim/bin/collect_swim.py]
interval = 3600
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:swim
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```
visualization: Table (deviceFamily, runningVersion, targetVersion, version_gap, non_compliant_count), single value (non-compliant device total), bar chart by deviceFamily.
knownFalsePositives:
**Device in the middle of a staged firmware upgrade showing temporary non-compliance.** During a SWIM-managed upgrade, devices stay marked NON_COMPLIANT until the upgrade completes and the device reboots onto the new image. Distinguish by checking `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim"` for `upgradeStatus=IN_PROGRESS` on the same device. Suppress by filtering `| where upgradeStatus!="IN_PROGRESS"` from non-compliance alerting.

**Pilot or canary devices intentionally running non-golden firmware for testing.** Devices in a firmware pilot ring may run a newer or different version than the golden image. This is by design. Distinguish by checking whether `runningVersion` is newer than `targetVersion` (not older). Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_swim_exceptions` lookup with pilot device names and approved firmware versions.

**Golden image target changed but compliance check not yet re-evaluated.** When the SWIM golden image is updated, all devices are re-evaluated. Until the next compliance scan completes (up to 1 hour), the non-compliance status reflects the old target. Distinguish by correlating with audit logs for golden image changes. Suppress by allowing one compliance scan cycle (3600s) after a golden image change before alerting.

**Device family not supported by SWIM reporting as non-compliant.** Some device families (e.g., older Cisco platforms) may not fully support SWIM image compliance checking, returning inconsistent results. Distinguish by checking whether the `deviceFamily` is known to have SWIM limitations. Suppress by filtering known-unsupported device families from the firmware compliance dashboard.

references.title: Catalyst Center Compliance API ‚Äî Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **swim** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`.
‚Ä¢ Catalyst Center SWIM golden images defined for each device family under Design > Image Repository.
‚Ä¢ RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** for SWIM compliance data.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
The TA polls the SWIM API at the configured interval. Each event includes the device's current firmware version and compliance status against the golden image.

Key fields for firmware non-compliance:
‚Ä¢ `upgradeStatus` or `complianceStatus`: "COMPLIANT", "NON_COMPLIANT", "IN_PROGRESS", "SCHEDULED".
‚Ä¢ `runningVersion`: current firmware version.
‚Ä¢ `targetVersion`: golden image version.
‚Ä¢ `deviceName`: device hostname.
‚Ä¢ `deviceFamily`: device platform family.
‚Ä¢ `platformId`: hardware PID.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim"
| stats latest(upgradeStatus) as compliance latest(runningVersion) as running latest(targetVersion) as target by deviceName, deviceFamily, platformId
| where compliance="NON_COMPLIANT"
| eval version_gap=running." ‚Üí ".target
| stats count as non_compliant_count values(version_gap) as version_gaps by deviceFamily
| sort -non_compliant_count
```

Understanding this SPL:
‚Ä¢ **`latest(upgradeStatus) as compliance`**: Takes the most recent compliance status per device, avoiding double-counting from multiple poll events.
‚Ä¢ **`where compliance="NON_COMPLIANT"`**: Filters to devices not running the golden image.
‚Ä¢ **`version_gap`**: Shows the running ‚Üí target version pair, making it easy to identify which specific versions need upgrading.
‚Ä¢ **`by deviceFamily`**: Groups non-compliance by device family for prioritized remediation.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Software Images. Check the compliance summary ‚Äî the count of non-compliant devices should match the Splunk results within ¬±2%.
‚Ä¢ **Device spot-check:** Pick a non-compliant device from the Splunk results. In Catalyst Center, verify its running version and target version match.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ **Dashboard:** Non-compliance count by device family (bar chart), compliance trend over 30 days (timechart), and a table of non-compliant devices sorted by device family.
‚Ä¢ **Alert:** Trigger when `non_compliant_count` increases by >10% in a single day ‚Äî indicates new non-compliance (possibly from a golden image change) requiring attention.
‚Ä¢ **Upgrade planning:** Use the version gap data to plan SWIM upgrade campaigns.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshoot
‚Ä¢ **All devices show NON_COMPLIANT after golden image change:** This is expected ‚Äî after updating the golden image, all devices on the old version become non-compliant. Allow one compliance scan cycle (3600s) before alerting.
‚Ä¢ **IN_PROGRESS devices not resolving:** Check SWIM task status in Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Tasks for upgrade failures.
‚Ä¢ **`upgradeStatus` field not present:** Try `complianceStatus` instead ‚Äî field naming varies by Catalyst Center version.
‚Ä¢ **No SWIM events arriving:** SWIM requires a custom scripted input; verify the script is deployed and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
‚Ä¢ **NULL or empty values in target version:** devices without a golden image assignment have no target; missing values indicate no SWIM policy is configured for that device family.
‚Ä¢ **Fewer devices than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We catch any device running the wrong software version compared to what your team has approved as the standard, which could mean security gaps.
